statement.markdown:
Given a Trie data structure, implement an insert method that inserts a word into the Trie. However, there is a bug in the implementation that occurs when the input word has a duplicate key. In such cases, the implementation should ignore the duplicate key and continue with the rest of the word.

Assumptions:
- Nodes in the Trie are initially empty.
- Each node has a set of children nodes (i.e., the 26 letters of the alphabet).
- Each node has a flag to mark the end of a word.

Your task is to fix the buggy insert method so that it correctly handles duplicate keys.

Example input:
3
hello
world
hello

Expected output:
true
true
false
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line containing a word to be inserted into the Trie.
statement.outputFormat: For each test case, print true if the word is inserted successfully, and false otherwise.
statement.notes: Note that the Trie is not provided in the input, but you should implement it according to the problem description.

examples.explanation: The output shows that the word 'hello' is inserted twice without any issues, but the second insertion of 'hello' is ignored because it's a duplicate key.

hints.text: Check the insert method to see where the bug is introduced.

editorial.title: Fixing Trie Insertion Edge Case
editorial.intuition: The bug is introduced when checking if a node already exists in the Trie.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would be to always insert a new node when checking for existing nodes.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to check if the node already exists and only insert a new node if it doesn't.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves checking if the node exists using a set to keep track of existing nodes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves demonstrating that the insert method correctly handles duplicate keys.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the corrected insert method.
